tokens: (Noticing the TV in motion) Hey, it moves!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, I'm going to get SRC 1.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, could we have a EMU check on you fellows when convenient?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I must be 80 percent (oxygen remaining) and no (warning) flags and no (warning) tones.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, LMP is about 80...Let me see.
(Counting marks on the gauge) 75...About 80 percent (oxygen), and no flags, no tones.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And you've sure got a lot of stuff on the Rover already.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, Mag Helen has just gone into (that is, under) the seat.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: They don't like to lock on this table very well.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and Jack, did you get mag...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And we did not copy your cuff gauge reading (suit pressure) down here.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, maybe that's because I didn't give it to you.
No wonder that's so much work.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, SRC 1 is...(Pause) She sure won't stay in the MESA very...(Pause) There, let me try that.
It sure doesn't seem like it wants to stay there, though.
And the organic sample has been sealed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Seeing the TV move) I guess you believe we're here now, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, the SRC cover will not stay closed.
There's nothing I can seem to do for it.
I might be able to set something - a blanket - on top or something.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm putting...(Responding to Bob) Yeah, it just flops open.
I'm taking SCB-1 to the Tool Gate (as per
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll get me a hammer and I'll give you a gravimeter reading.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll go get the (U.S.) flag (out of the MESA) then.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The Buddy SLSS is on the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, ETB...Okay, CDR's camera film magazine I had to work on a little bit to get it to work, but it's working.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Gene) If I get that camera (that is, Gene's camera, which is on his Rover seat), you can punch the gravimeter, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Get the camera, and I'll give them a gravimeter reading.
Because I'll go get the (U.S.) flag (as per
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you'd better let that...Yeah, but why don't when you go...Let me get some tongs, too, (off the gate).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me steady the Rover and punch (the gravimeter button).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Mark gravimeter and the light is flashing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Singing) "Oh, bury me not on the lone prairie.
Where the coyotes howl, and the wind blows free." Okay, where am I?
(More to himself than to Gene) You're doing a gravimeter (and) getting the flag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That reminds me of a good book.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't go near the Rover?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't go near the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How about you letting me stick these in your pocket with your (hammer)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pointing into the MESA) Set them up there.
We'll get them when we come back in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll tell you what I'm going to do.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Put them in the...Gonna hang them here on the (ladder) hook.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How about the flag right over here in this little mound?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, let me take a look over here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about right up there on that little high point?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Right up in here where I'm going.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Of course, your idea of a high point might be different than mine.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's probably the best place in the world for the flag, is right up on the top.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me come over and help you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Laughing as he thinks about the possibility of hitting bedrock with the flag pole) (Garbled) how much regolith (soil) we've got in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, you're in the edge of the crater though.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Move right over here near your tire tracks.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This is a high point right here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me give it a few whacks.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Want to make sure it stands up.
That's getting pretty...What we could do...I don't know how far we could drill...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we hit something solid with that one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, but did you ever see it vibrate like that?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, I've never put a flag up on the Moon before.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughing) You're going to have to get it down to my level.
You tall guys are all alike.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How about getting it stretched out?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I just can't start forward as fast as I'd like to.
(Pause) Hate to touch it, my hands are so dirty.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, it's going to want to curl.
Maybe it'll...It sort of looks like it's waving in the breeze.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) (We'll) take a couple (of pictures) this way, and we'll take a couple that way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, let me get over to the other side.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We can get the Rover in the background.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It does wave when you do that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We've got a beautiful picture of you guys up (on the screen) down there (in the front of the Control Room).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: This flag is a beautiful picture.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, it's partially covering the Rover, but I think it's a pretty good shot.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get the focus right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know how to put it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: All right, I got you reaching for the flag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get it in stereo.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...this has got to be one of the most proud moments of my life.
(Pause) (To Jack) Why don't you get a close-in one and we'll trade cameras.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Houston, I don't know how many of you are aware of this, but this flag has flown in the MOCR (Mission Operations Control Room at the Johnson Space Center in Houston) since Apollo 11.

And we very proudly deploy it on the Moon, to stay for as long as it can, in honor of all those people who have worked so hard to put us here and to put every other crew here and to make the country, United States, and mankind, something different than it was.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And presuming to speak in behalf of some of those that work on the MOCR, we thank you very much.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm going to get on the other side.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I want to get something here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I want to get the Earth.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't think it's going...You're a little close, maybe, to have them both in focus.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Try that one time, then we'll give up and get to work.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get over here closer to you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You think your gravimeter's ready so I can go back there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The gravimeter's ready and a couple of words here.
One, I presume you found the
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And the second thing is: we do want the SRC closed.
And if you can partially latch it, I'm not sure that's easily done, that would be one solution.
The other would be to put something on top of it to hold it closed.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: One of the brackets off the MESA would be something, or a rock that's nearby; that's another possibility.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Gene, what are you going to be up to now?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to go get the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Starting toward the LM) Okay, why don't I give the old (LM) inspection bit here.
(Goes back to the Rover) And I really ought to have my camera, shouldn't I?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll take the old CDR's camera.
Not a bad camera to take.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, I'm going to take the old gunny sack here and put it over (the SRC).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I presume you're talking about the big bag, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, the big bag that was on the ladder hook.
There's just enough spring force in it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think you've had all the good words about the LM.
We have never flown a better LM.
(Chuckles) I guess that's safe enough to say.
The old steerables are aimed right at you.
It's parked, looks like parallel to Z (that is, east-west).
There's no visible...I'm on the 3 o'clock position,
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How's that for coming down gentle?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: That's what you call "Okay, Number Three Wire", Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, we never heard what our landing parameters were.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't really care, now that we're here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, but they always give them to us in the simulator.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Bob, judging from what I see on my clock (the stopwatch function on his wristwatch), we're not but about 5 minutes behind.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That agrees more or less with the way we read it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Gene had a little forward motion as I think you heard us call.
And that shows up in the forward footpad at any rate, or did (before the EVA started and Gene and Jack scuffed up the mark).
It looks like he may have hit tail first a little bit.
That's embedded to the full pad depth.
I see no...By George, Gene, you may have had a first.
I think you stroked that thing.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, we can measure it and find out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll take a picture of it (and save time by letting the folks back in Houston settle the question after the mission.)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The lower orange Mylar, is folded a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's word floating around down here about typical Navy landings, but I'm not sure whether we believe it or not.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Just behind the LM, in that fairly fresh crater, I picked up an example of the kind of gabbro I was talking about.
And I'll stick it in the big bag, except the big bag has disappeared.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) Okay, I've got to give you a (gravimeter) reading, Bob, if you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You put the big bag up...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, I put that (big bag) there to hold the SRC down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's all right, I just put a sample in it.
It's in the bottom of the bag.
It's about 8 by 5 centimeters by 3 centimeters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got to tell you, Bob.
I haven't done everything there is to do in the Navy, but deploying that flag has got to be the most proud thing I'll ever do in my life.
If you could see you (the Earth), and you could see it (the flag) from where we are, I know you'd feel the same way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: God, you're pretty up there over the South Massif.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You weren't doing anything with the gravimeter on here (that is, on the Rover), I hope.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, it's on the deck (that is, on the ground).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you might grab me a frame count when you set it on there, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, now if I can figure how to get this off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) You've got to educate us again.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, here's a temperature for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, the "Shade" is deployed facing deep space.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's not on the post yet, but it's deployed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Don't get all caught on something.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think (I've) just about got the "Sun" side deployed, just as perpendicular to the Sun as I think anybody could do.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I don't have any pictures (of the cosmic ray experiment) yet (because Jack doesn't have a camera with him), so you might put that down as something to get later.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, we'll catch that in the pan(orama) next EVA or something like that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Boy, if that (SEP) antenna doesn't get some noise from outer space, I don't know what will.
If they are out there, and they are I'm sure.
That is even weirder looking out here than it is in the high bay.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Before I leave (with) the ALSEP, remind me to check the cosmic ray.
I might hit it here in the process of deployment.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Responding to Bob's "Try not to") Oh, I will.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know what talent you have for landing in holes, Cernan, but once again I'll be doing all the ALSEP work in a hole.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Are you saying we should have kept the pulleys there, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Agreeing with Bob, but grudgingly) Yeah, I need the pulleys.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You know, Bob, I've got a little bit of a problem here.
I've got the SEP connector on.
(Jack laughs) But...It'll slide down in, but the locking cover just won't go over.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And it slides in far enough, and you think it's aligned, huh?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It just didn't appear to lock over, well not appear, it just won't lock over.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm glad we have the right solution to that one, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The right solution is the fact that you've got a man here doing it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The ECA Temp Monitor switch is On.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's an easy way and a hard to do everything.
Don't know why we don't pick the easy way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Central station is...Hey, Bob; Gene's little pitch-up makes these things slide out by themselves almost.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Better thank him next time you see him.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Hey, Jack, you notice there's none of those guys up there holding those hoses as we go around the LM?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I saw one just a minute ago.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Long Pause) Just took time out for a snack and a little water ( meaning a
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) To coin a phrase, it's a 'panoramic scene of beauty'.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Say, Bob, what do you think of the terrain?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's why you're...(Laughs; Gene has beaten him to the joke).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I'll give you a reading on the
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Talking to himself) Don't kick dust on it.
Hope I can read it down here.
(Pause) Bob, you're going to have to bear with me.
When I leaned over to punch it, I hit Gravity instead of Read, so I guess I got to wait it out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I should have been more careful.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The SRC doesn't have to be all the way closed does it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just as long as it's most of the way closed.
You can have a crack there in the top.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: This thing (the MESA) got low all of a sudden.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You get it (the RTG) fueled yet?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me know if you have any problems with that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, that gravimeter went right to...It blinked once and went right to steady, so I don't expect it'll be too long.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I'll give you a call in a couple of minutes there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I understand you have the RTG fueled?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm supposed to call you when I have it fueled.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Am I missing a map I should have up here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I put them in there so they wouldn't bounce off.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I wish I could go back and make that landing about 6 or 7 times so I could take in all that I missed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I might as well have stayed at the Cape.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Geno, you might wander by the gravimeter.
I think it might be done by now.
You might just check the light and see if it's steady, or on or not.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll go by there right now, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: For future reference, Bob, the dome removal tool (pause) doesn't...It'll turn.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If the light's out, give us a reading.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, let's see if I can punch the right button this time.
And it was about 75 percent in the shade of the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And now we're ready for "Bias" (as per
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Now, you want...(Listens) Okay, a "Bias" coming at you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've got the core bag and the neutron flux, and...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, the dome removal tool never latched into the
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me have your hammer because I'm going to have to pry off the dome.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I......You see I...I've stripped it, I think.
I didn't think I could do it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Wait a minute, wait a minute, wait a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just put your blade in there.
Put the blade in there; and pry.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Here, let me get it once from this side.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Once again we have the right solution.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm not sure, Bob, what happened.
You might ask them that if you only partially get the dome removal tool on, if you can strip the whole thing out?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It won't make much difference any more.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll make sure it's changed on the next dome removal tool.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I'm just taking a breather.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That was a strange one, Gene.
Did you see how I mangled that thing?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got to go back and get the
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I'll give you my word.
Before we leave here, I'll make sure that the SRC is closed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: As long as it's got only an inch or two showing there, it should be no problem.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Man, I've got to put something on it to get it down to that far.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I'm checking out the cosmic ray.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I snuck a queek...quick peek at the drill, and it does work.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Hearing a burst of radio noise) What in the world is that?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Got his VHF on, that fink.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Houston) Hey, you might tell Ron we can hear him.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: "Drill to LMP seat." With seat belt.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, ALSEP is put together in the barbell mode.
And Charlie Duke, I have checked it; and it is locked.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you read, we're reading you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Picking up the ALSEP package; singing) Well, "We're off to see the Wizard."
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, do you need me, Gene?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to go deploy an ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: First, I've got to find an ALSEP site.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I'll get that (fender) in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm moving down-Sun (west), and where we've walked, we stir up darker material - just slightly (darker) - but it's darker.
The same old thing that most regoliths have.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Have you got a bias reading there, Gene?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, I'm giving it to you right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I hate to say it, but I'm going to have to take some time to try...I'm going to have to try to get that fender back on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Was it the rear fender, Geno?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Caught it with my hammer, and it just popped right off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, for future reference, it's a piece of cake putting the
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack, is the tape under my seat, do you remember?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Man, you're wobbling around like a...How are you doing?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's just...It's work going out here!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to be a little bit behind you (on the timeline) if I have to work on that fender, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, you can walk a bit more slowly than you're walking, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, more and more...(Hearing Bob) What's that?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I said that you can walk more slowly than you started out, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Breathing heavily) Bob, texturally, some of these rocks that I believe are the gabbros have a texture not unlike a welded tuff (a rock made from volcanic ash which fuses because of its own internal heat).
But they've got some mottled characteristic to them that I haven't yet figured out.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, if it wasn't for that fender, I'd be ready to go.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I say there, Jack, that looks like a big rock there beyond you.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: That's the one we were talking about.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I've done this in training.
I can't say I'm very adept at putting fenders back on.
But I sure don't want to start without it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think I'm going to move a little bit to the northwest of my present position in order to get a little farther away from that big rock (Geophone Rock).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And to get out of a shallow depression...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm in a shallow depression that's here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You disappeared out of sight from the last (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well it's shallow relative to other depressions I've been in.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll be able to...There are enough rocks around.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And Gene, if you're having trouble with that fender and you think it might be easier with two guys, you could wait until you get out to the ALSEP site.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No sir, I got it on, but a little piece of the rail is cracked off.
And I'm just going to put a couple of pieces of good old-fashioned American gray tape on it...(and) see whether we can't make sure it stays.
Because I don't want to lose it.
(Pause) Except good old-fashioned gray tape doesn't want to stick very well (because of the dust).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've not seen any sign of layering in any of the craters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The rocks still seem to be the pinkish-gray gabbro out here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Good old-fashioned American gray tape doesn't stick to lunar-dust-covered fenders.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think it'll stay, for an indefinite period of time, right now.
(Pause) (Tearing a piece of tape off the roll) Not bad for EV gloves.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You disappeared out of sight a long while ago.
(Pause) Hey, you just came into sight again, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, leave me enough room to deploy the heat flow.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to, I'm looking for a place.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's why I didn't land up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I think I've got a place.
And I think it'll also give you a spot for the neutron flux that's sheltered from the RTG.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You say you have a place like that, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, pretty much, I think, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me work on it here a little more.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and right now you're about 10 minutes behind the timeline, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I'm only going to spend another minute or two on the fender.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I never thought I'd be out here doing this.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Turning south) Okay, I'm going to go back this way.
Central Station can be near a crater.
(Pause) It will be pretty good, that'll put the LEAM (Lunar Ejecta and Meteorite Experiment) right out over there (25 feet southeast of the Central Station) which is probably all right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The gravimeter out over there (25 feet west of the Central Station), which is probably all right.
Going to put your drill holes a little too close to that rock, though.
Bob, ask Mark if he's worried about rocks as much as craters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got a rock about 2 meters in diameter, partially buried, that one of the probes (the westerly probe, as it will turn out) may be near.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I can move a little more south, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And Jack, it seems (that), if you're about 3 meters from the rock, that's no problem.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause; as he moves south about 15 to 20 feet) Okay, this is it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I tell you, the block (Geophone Rock)...(Pause) Let's see, the Sun's (garbled), this way.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, it looks like the (heat flow) probes are going to be in a shallow depression.
I'll try to improve that a little.
It's not a real crater; it's just a shallow depression.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Stand by on that, Jack, a minute.
Okay, shallow depression's all right, Jack, don't worry about it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's not more than a meter deep.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It looks pretty good to me.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, it's really, in detail...The meter and half-meter scale relief is a little more than we can stand here for a good site.
But I think this will be all right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're ready to press on with ALSEP interconnect.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And Geno, how are you doing on that fender?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: If that fender stays on...I'm going to take a picture of it because I'd like some sort of mending award.
It's not too neat, but tape and lunar dust just don't hang in there together.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Keep your fingers crossed, and I'll be more careful around the fenders.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I'm going to do one other thing real quick here.
I've got to dust my visor off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gene, do you want me to do that?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll just do it right here.
Only have to do it in a couple of places right in front of me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That didn't do much good, did it.
Someone should have told me that.
That's just really screwed it up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, you might ought to be thinking of a good way to clean that visor when I get in the cabin.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, we'll put someone on that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm ready to drive to the ALSEP site.
Still want to park 60 - east and north.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Getting on the Rover) Boy, oh boy.
You just got to be careful where you kick dust.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, Bob, I thought they (the battery temperatures) were a little high, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Batt Temps are 100 and 120, right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about tapping the meter a little bit for us?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I think the meter's been tapped since we've been working on the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to be heading west.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Jack, I'm on the way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
